title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 9 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a market notice on 9 February 2026 announcing its first foreign exchange reserve financing operation of the year under an updated issuance programme announced in September 2025. The operation involves a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond, with RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley appointed as Joint Lead Managers. Execution is subject to market conditions.
key_points: Bank of England shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually for foreign exchange reserve financing, announced September 2025
First 2026 operation is a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
Transaction execution is subject to market conditions
BoE foreign exchange reserves are separate from Government's own foreign exchange reserves
Updated programme features regular timetable and high degree of transparency
why_it_matters: This market notice operationalizes the Bank of England's restructured foreign exchange reserve financing programme announced September 2025. The shift to predictable, transparent benchmark issuances (twice annually) affects market structure by providing regular anticipated supply signals to USD bond markets, reducing uncertainty around central bank reserve management operations, and establishing consistent distribution infrastructure through joint lead managers.
macro_context: The Bank of England's foreign exchange reserves support policy objectives. USD benchmark bond issuance by a major central bank contributes to sovereign debt supply in USD markets. The two annual issuances create a regular, anticipated supply cadence.
market_structure_context: The transition from irregular reserve financing to a structured programme with two annual benchmark issuances represents a structural change in BoE's balance sheet management and market communication. Using four joint lead managers (RBC, BMO, HSBC, Morgan Stanley) establishes distribution infrastructure for reserve-linked securities. The programme's high transparency and regular timetable reduce information asymmetry in USD bond markets.
